We often pray for mercy when we seek it; and should we only give thanks once or twice when we obtain it? God shows that he is near to us in what we ask him for. Public responsibilities are to be managed honestly. This can certainly apply to Christ and his rule. Human sin threatened to destroy the entire creation, but Christ saved the world from complete ruin. He who is made of God to us wisdom tells us to be wise. To proud, bold sinners he says, do not boast of your power, do not continue in contempt. All present hopes and future happiness of the human race come from the Son of God.

No secondary factors will elevate people without the First Cause. It comes neither from the east, nor from the west, nor from the south. He does not mention the north; the same word that signifies the north also signifies the secret place; and it comes from the secret of God’s counsel. From God alone all must receive their fate. There are mixtures of mercy and grace in the cup of affliction when it is put into the hands of God's people; mixtures of the curse when it is put into the hands of the wicked. God’s people have their share in common calamities, but the dregs of the cup are for the wicked. The exaltation of the Son of David will be the subject of the saints’ everlasting praises. Then let sinners submit to the King of righteousness, and let believers rejoice in and obey him.
